title: Run K-fold cross-validated HB fits against a `choice-cv split` directory
summary: Run Hierarchical Bayes fits across a directory of cross-validation folds. Each fold subdirectory must contain train.csv and test.csv (the layout produced by `choice-cv split`). Writes per-fold runs under run_dir/<fold_id>/ and an aggregate cv_summary.csv summarising train/holdout fit across folds.
description: Walks a `choice-cv split` directory (one subdirectory per fold, each containing `train.csv` and `test.csv`) and runs an HB fit on every fold. The hyperparameters supplied apply to all folds — the fold structure itself is determined entirely by the input directory layout. After all folds complete, an aggregate `cv_summary.csv` is written to the top of `run_dir` with mean and standard deviation of train and holdout log-likelihood across folds.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "<cv-folds dir>"
run_dir = "<output dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
# optional:
segments = 2
iterations = 10000
threads = 4
random_seed = 0
burnin = 0.2
covariate_input = "<covariates.csv>"

arguments.name: folds_dir
arguments.description: Directory containing per-fold subdirectories. Each subdirectory must contain `train.csv` and `test.csv` files in coded long format. This is exactly the layout `choice-cv::split` produces.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Created if it does not exist. Each fold gets its own subdirectory under `run_dir/` (named after the input fold), and `cv_summary.csv` plus `cv_summary.json` are written at the top level after all folds complete.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the per-fold CSVs have a header row. Default `true`.

arguments.name: segments
arguments.description: Number of mixture segments. Default `2`. Applied to every fold.

arguments.name: iterations
arguments.description: Total MCMC iterations per fold. Default `10000`. The same iteration budget is used for every fold; if a single fold needs more, use `cv-fit-extend` after the run.

arguments.name: save_every
arguments.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default `10`.

arguments.name: checkpoint_every
arguments.description: Write checkpoint every N saved draws. Default `100`.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of threads per fold. Default `4`. Folds are processed sequentially, so the overall thread count never exceeds this value.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed (`0` = non-deterministic). Default `0`. Each fold derives its own seed from the base seed; with the same base seed and the same input directory the entire CV run is reproducible.

arguments.name: chains
arguments.description: Number of independent MCMC chains per fold. Default `1`. Each chain uses a seed derived from `random_seed` and its chain index. Draws are pooled at finalize time within each fold.

arguments.name: report_thin
arguments.description: Convergence-report snapshot interval in saved draws per fold. Default `0` (same as `checkpoint_every`). Controls chart data-point density.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction for per-fold holdout evaluation. Default `0.2`. Applied uniformly to every fold's chain when computing holdout LL.

arguments.name: amu
arguments.description: Precision of normal prior on segment means. Default `0.01`.

arguments.name: nu_inc
arguments.description: Degrees-of-freedom increment for covariance prior. Default `3`.

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: Dirichlet concentration per segment. Length must equal `segments`.

arguments.name: covariate_prior_scale
arguments.description: Prior scale for covariate regression coefficients. Default `0.01`.

arguments.name: step_size
arguments.description: MH proposal step size. Omit for automatic tuning per fold.

arguments.name: init_ridge
arguments.description: Numerical ridge for Hessian during MAP init. Default `1e-8`.

arguments.name: init_iterations
arguments.description: Max iterations for MAP initialisation. Default `250`.

arguments.name: attribute_signs
arguments.description: Sign constraints applied to every fold. Format: `[{ name = "price", rule = "non_pos" }, ...]` with `rule` ∈ `{non_neg, non_pos}`.

arguments.name: covariate_input
arguments.description: Optional respondent-level covariate CSV (`resp_id` + numeric columns). Same file used by every fold; the per-fold train/test split is at the *response* level, not the covariate level, so the covariate matrix carries through unchanged.

details:
`cv-fit` is the standard way to get cross-validated holdout log-likelihood from an HB model. It assumes you have already run `choice-cv::split` to produce a directory of per-fold train / test pairs; this command then runs `fit` against each of them with a shared hyperparameter set.

Folds are processed sequentially. Within each fold, `threads` worker threads parallelise the per-respondent likelihood evaluation, but no two folds run at the same time — this keeps memory usage bounded. The whole run can take a while; budget for at least `K * single-fit-time` where `K` is the fold count.

Once every fold has finished, `cv-fit` writes an aggregate summary (`cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json`) at the top of `run_dir` with mean and standard deviation of the per-fold train and holdout log-likelihood. This is the headline cross-validation metric for downstream model comparison.

If the run finishes but some folds undershoot, use `cv-fit-extend` to add more iterations to every fold without redoing the warm-up work. To explore a different hyperparameter set, run a fresh `cv-fit` against a new `run_dir`.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:

- One subdirectory per fold (named to match the input fold), each containing the same layout as a single `fit` run (chain chunks, `spec.json`, `exports/`, etc.).
- `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json` at the top level: per-fold and aggregate train / holdout log-likelihood and acceptance statistics.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then per-fold start / progress / finish events, then a final `event.run.finished` carrying the aggregate summary.
notes: Folds are processed sequentially — total wall-clock time scales linearly with fold count.
Use `cv-fit-extend` to add more iterations to every fold if the initial run undershoots; don't re-run from scratch.
The `covariate_input` file is shared across folds; respondents that belong to a fold's test set still get their covariates from this file.

see_also.description: Produces the per-fold directory layout this command consumes.
Extend every fold of an existing CV run.
Re-summarise an existing CV run with a different burnin.
Single-fit entry point — use this for non-CV runs.
Latent-class counterpart — useful as a comparison baseline.
Aggregate MNL counterpart — useful as a fast lower-complexity baseline.

examples.title: Long CV run with sign constraint and reproducible seed
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-cv-long"
run_name = "hbmh_run2"
iterations = 20000
attribute_signs = [{ name = "price", rule = "non_pos" }]
random_seed = 42

examples.title: 3-segment CV with respondent covariates
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-cv-3seg"
run_name = "hbmh_run3"
segments = 3
iterations = 15000
covariate_input = "./data/respondent_covariates.csv"
random_seed = 7

fields.name: folds_dir
fields.description: Directory containing per-fold subdirs (each with train.csv + test.csv).

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory. Per-fold subdirs and cv_summary.csv are written here.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether CSVs have a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Number of mixture segments. Default: 2.

fields.name: iterations
fields.description: Total MCMC iterations per fold. Default: 10000.

fields.name: save_every
fields.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default: 10.

fields.name: checkpoint_every
fields.description: Write checkpoint every N saved draws. Default: 100.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Number of threads per fold. Default: 4.

fields.name: random_seed
fields.description: Random seed (0 = non-deterministic). Default: 0.

fields.name: chains
fields.description: Number of independent MCMC chains per fold. Default: 1.

fields.name: report_thin
fields.description: Snapshot every N saved draws per fold (0 = checkpoint_every). Default: 0.

fields.name: burnin
fields.description: Burnin fraction for per-fold holdout eval. Default: 0.2.

fields.name: amu
fields.description: Precision of normal prior on segment means. Default: 0.01.

fields.name: nu_inc
fields.description: Degrees-of-freedom increment for covariance prior. Default: 3.

fields.name: a
fields.description: Dirichlet concentration per segment (length = segments).

fields.name: covariate_prior_scale
fields.description: Prior scale for covariate regression coefficients. Default: 0.01.

fields.name: step_size
fields.description: MH proposal step size. Omit for automatic tuning.

fields.name: init_ridge
fields.description: Numerical ridge for Hessian during MAP init. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: init_iterations
fields.description: Max iterations for MAP initialisation. Default: 250.

fields.name: attribute_signs
fields.description: Sign constraints: [{name, rule}] where rule is non_neg or non_pos.

fields.name: covariate_input
fields.description: Optional covariate CSV (resp_id + numeric columns).

artifacts.title: Cross-validation summary
artifacts.description: Per-fold and aggregate holdout prediction accuracy. The headline metric for comparing HB against latent class and aggregate logit on the same data — a higher average holdout score indicates better generalisation.

fields.name: fold
fields.description: Fold identifier.

fields.name: train_fit
fields.description: In-sample fit statistic for the fold.

fields.name: holdout_fit
fields.description: Out-of-sample prediction accuracy for the fold.

artifacts.title: Per-fold exports
artifacts.description: Each fold subdirectory contains the same outputs as a single `fit` run: partworths and segment summary for that fold's training sample.
